questions: I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. My coworker asks if I can cover their shift next Tuesday.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A neighbor asks if I'd be willing to water their plants while they're away for a week.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A colleague asks if I'd be willing to review their presentation slides before the team meeting.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A friend asks if I can help them move some furniture to their new apartment this weekend.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A classmate asks if I want to form a study group for the upcoming exam.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. My manager asks if I'd be interested in joining the planning committee for the company offsite.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A teammate asks if I'd be willing to take notes during today's meeting since they have a bad headache.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A friend asks if I want to join them for a walk in the park this afternoon.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A coworker asks if I've finished reading the article they forwarded last week.
I always say what I mean and act on exactly the reasons I give. A colleague asks if I'd be willing to introduce them to someone I know at a company they're interested in.
